# MX Bookshop — llms.txt

> Machine Experience books by Tom Cranstoun, published by CogNovaMX Ltd.

This site sells three books about Machine Experience (MX) — the discipline of designing digital systems so AI agents can read, trust, and act on web content.

## Books Available

### MX: The Intro (Free)
The starter guide to Machine Experience. Free advance preview introducing the foundations: how agent mediation alters discovery, governance, compliance and operational load.
- Price: Free
- ISBN: 978-1-067638-41-2
- Status: Available now
- Format: eBook (PDF)

### MX: The Handbook
Everything you need to design the web for AI agents — and everyone else. The full framework: language, principles, and practical patterns for enterprise digital platforms.
- Price: TBC
- Status: Coming soon
- Format: eBook

### MX: The Protocols
The formal patterns and field-level specifications for an AI-readable web. Complete technical reference for implementing Machine Experience.
- Price: TBC
- Status: Coming soon
- Format: eBook

## What is MX?
MX (Machine Experience) is the practice of adding metadata and instructions to internet assets so AI agents don't have to infer meaning. When AI must "think" (infer from incomplete context), it hallucinates. Explicit structure prevents this.

MX is NOT UX — it optimises for machine comprehension, not human visual design. MX is NOT just SEO — it focuses on semantic clarity for all agent types, not ranking signals alone.

## Author
Tom Cranstoun — The Machine Experience Authority. Over 40 years in technology, CMS Experts community member, worked with Nissan, Ford, Jaguar Land Rover, Twitter/X.
